name: Hootsuite
price_basis: Standard at $99 per user per month based on annual billing, one seat, checked 2026-07
verdict_reason: KILL: $1,188 a year for one seat. Enterprise rent for scheduling posts a cron job sends for free.
what_zo_builds: The same queue build that replaces Buffer, minus the enterprise chrome: per-channel queue files, a cron publisher, a log of every post, and a weekly digest. Basic stats come straight from each platform's own endpoints into one report you actually read. No seats, no per-user math, no dashboard rent.
dont_kill_if: A team shares the calendar with approval chains, or you run paid social and community inboxes across brands. That workflow is what the seat price is for.
build_brief: Goal: replace my Hootsuite seat with a scheduler I own. Export scheduled posts, connected accounts, and saved drafts. Build per-channel queue files and a cron publisher that posts through each platform's API and records results. Pull basic per-post stats from the platforms into a weekly report. Parallel-run for two weeks, since the price is steep and the workflows are stickier, then cancel the seat. Done when every queued post ships on time from my own queue and the $99 seat is gone.
